variants.title: Filtrec A142C10 Spin-On Filter
variants.description: Genuine Filtrec A142C10. This Filtrec element is designed to meet or exceed the quality and performance of brand OEM’s ensuring superior filtration performance and long-lasting durability. Engineered with their high performance media and a durable design, the A142C10 provides reliable and consistent performance over its lifespan, even in harsh operating environments. When you order from FilterFinder, you can trust that you are getting a reliable and high-quality replacement filter that will keep your system running smoothly.
specifications: Style: Spin-On Filter, Media: Cellulose, Efficiency 1: 10µ @ β2, Outer Diameter: 96.0 mm, Length: 210.0 mm, Thread: 1 1/2"-16 UN, Δ Collapse Pressure: 17 bar, Seal Material: Nitrile, Temperature Range: -30 - +100°C, Efficiency 1 The efficiency of the element is shown using the Micron (µ) and Beta (β) ratings. The micron rating indicates the size of the particle being filtered and the beta rating the efficiency , calculated as 1/β. E.g. a rating of β200 will be 99.5% efficient whereas β1000 would be 99.9%.: 10µ @ β2, Media Type: Cellulose, Collapse Pressure: 17 BAR, Seal: Nitrile

variants.title: Donaldson P550705 Hydraulic Filter, Spin-On | Replaces the Filtrec A142C10
variants.option1Value: Donaldson P550705 Hydraulic Filter, Spin-On | Replaces the Filtrec A142C10
variants.description: Genuine Donaldson P550705 Hydraulic Filter, Spin-On provides dependable protection for hydraulic systems by efficiently removing particulates and contaminants. It ensures clean hydraulic fluid, promoting smooth operation and extending the lifespan of critical system components. With its easy-to-install spin-on design, this filter minimizes downtime and maintenance costs. Its durable construction and advanced filtration media make it a trusted choice for industrial and mobile hydraulic applications requiring reliable performance.

The micron rating indicates the size of the particle being filtered and the beta rating the efficiency , calculated as 1/β.
